mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.11% (near zero lower bound) combined with rising CPI at 231.485 signals monetary policy is accommodative but inflation is accelerating. This real rate compression drives expectations of future rate hikes, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as investors demand higher compensation for inflation risk over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates deeply negative at decision point
regime_hint: post-crisis accommodation with emerging inflation pressure
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 20-day forward window despite trigger activation in > 40% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 7.6% remains elevated relative to natural rate estimates (5.0-5.5%), signaling labor market slack. Persistent slack suppresses wage growth expectations and inflation expectations, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to decline as investors reduce long-term inflation premium over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.6% is 200-250bp above estimated NAIRU
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of 20-day forward periods when UNRATE > 7.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales at 421,147 million dollars show consumer spending resilience. Strong demand growth signals the economy is operating near or above potential output, reducing slack and increasing inflation risk. The Fed will need to maintain or raise rates, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales growth indicates strong consumer demand
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 50% of triggered instances over 20-day horizon, or Rank IC < 0.06 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 98.67 (index 2017=100) is below trend, indicating supply-side weakness. Constrained supply combined with strong demand (RSAFS) creates inflationary pressure. Expectations of higher inflation cause the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as investors demand higher real yields over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 45% of triggered instances, or the supply-demand imbalance fails to predict yield direction with Rank IC > 0.08.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 853,000 units annualized show residential construction momentum. Strong housing demand signals robust economic activity and future inflation from construction wage pressures and material costs. The 10-year Treasury yield rises as investors price in higher long-term inflation expectations over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts above 800k indicate strong construction demand
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 50% of 60-day forward periods when HOUST > 800k, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 135,637 thousand shows continued job growth. Sustained employment growth tightens the labor market, raising wage growth expectations and inflation expectations. Higher inflation expectations cause the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as investors demand higher compensation for inflation risk over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong monthly payroll growth indicates tight labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of 5-day forward periods following strong payroll reports, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP at 13,746.2 billion (chained 2017 dollars) combined with elevated payroll employment and retail sales indicates the economy is in late-cycle expansion. Late-cycle dynamics produce inflation acceleration, forcing the Fed to signal tighter policy. The 10-year Treasury yield rises as investors anticipate higher future short rates over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized AND PAYEMS growth > 150k/month AND RSAFS growth > 0.4%
evidence_refs: fact:Multiple demand indicators signal late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 50% of 60-day forward periods when all three triggers are active, or the joint signal fails to predict yield direction with Rank IC > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.11% is at the zero lower bound while CPI is rising and unemployment is elevated at 7.6%. This regime of accommodative policy with rising inflation but slack labor market is unsustainable. Market expectations shift toward future tightening, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as investors price in the end of accommodation over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Unsustainable policy mix signals regime transition
regime_hint: transition from post-crisis accommodation to normalization
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 45% of triggered 20-day forward periods, or the regime-transition signal fails to predict yield direction with Rank IC < 0.07 over rolling 2-year windows.
